episodes.title: Meet the healers who say they have a 'gift from God' | Unreported World
episodes.description:
Unreported World is from the Philippines investigating the controversial world of spiritual healers. Sahar Zand examines whether the popular practice is a blessing or a curse.
 
Across the country, with high medical costs and difficulties accessing healthcare, patients put their trust in alternative treatments to cure anything from chronic pain to cancer. Faith healers, blending Catholic rituals with centuries-old traditions, promise miraculous recoveries through prayer and herbal remedies.
 
On the outskirts of Manilla, we meet three faith healers claiming to treat over one hundred people per week. They believe they are divinely chosen to cure conditions modern medicine can’t detect. Their ritualistic, sometimes disturbing rituals, have earned them a loyal following, and an income derived from donations, socials media followers and faith healing accessories. 
 
But critics warn the industry is dangerously unregulated. Medical professionals fear patients are being misled, delaying vital treatment with often fatal consequences. Zand hears from those who believe their loved ones paid the ultimate price, after putting their trust in miracle cures that they now believe were a scam.

And a warning, there are some scenes of ritualistic ceremonies, some viewers may find upsetting.

Director: Wil Davies
Picture Editor: Claire Pringle
Series Producer: Andy Lee
Executive Producer: Ed Fraser
Production Company: Channel 4 News

Subscribe to our channel for more Unreported World episodes https://www.youtube.com/unreportedworld.

episodes.title: Inside Kenya’s Christian Death Cult | Unreported World
episodes.description:
Unreported World investigates how one influential preacher in Kenya could have led hundreds of men, women and children to their deaths. Symeon Brown tracks down the survivors and the families searching for loved ones to find out if this was mass suicide, or mass murder.  

So far 429 bodies have been exhumed from graves in Shakahola forest where Pastor Paul Mackenzie’s followers are believed to have starved to death. The leader of the so-called death cult is said to have promised his followers protection from the 'coming apocalypse'. Mackenzie denies any wrongdoing. 

Meanwhile another church, Rainbow Ministries, is facing a crackdown for cult-like behaviour. But where is the line between faith and extremism? 

Producer/Director: Tom Besley
Series Producer: Andy Lee
Executive Producer: Ed Fraser
Production Company: Channel 4 News

Subscribe to our channel for more Unreported World episodes https://www.youtube.com/unreportedworld.
